title: Reading and Use of English – Part 3
description: For questions 17–24, read the text below. Use the word given in CAPITALS at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

content.text: Cooking is an (0) ENJOYABLE activity that allows people to express their creativity. To master the art of gastronomy, one needs (18) and a willingness to experiment with flavors. Many chefs emphasize that the (19) of ingredients is the key to a successful dish. Adding a (20) of herbs can drastically change the profile of a simple meal. (21), even professional cooks make mistakes, but these can lead to (22) culinary breakthroughs. The (23) of televised cooking shows has encouraged more people to cook at home. This (24) has fostered a deeper appreciation for diverse cuisines and traditional techniques. As individuals gain skills, they find that preparing food is not just a daily chore, but a satisfying way to share culture and love with friends and family. Ultimately, the kitchen becomes a place of constant learning and discovery, where every mistake is just a step toward a better meal.

example.explanation: Adjective required to modify 'activity'; ENJOY → ENJOYABLE.

questions.explanation: Noun required after the verb 'needs'; PATIENT → PATIENCE.
Noun required after the definite article; FRESH → FRESHNESS.
Adverb required at the start of the sentence; OCCASION → OCCASIONALLY.
Adjective with a negative prefix required; EXPECT → UNEXPECTED.
Noun required after the demonstrative 'this'; MOVE → MOVEMENT.
Adjective required to describe the people (implied); SKILL → SKILLFUL.
